assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
Outputs reflect correct relational encoding for typical non‑edge values
Exactly one of A_greater, A_equal, A_less is high for any legal input pair
cases.goal: Validate combinational correctness across representative mid‑range values
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Ensures mutual exclusivity of the three outputs
Confirms basic functional mapping without assuming latency
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['A', 'B']
Apply a set of mid‑range values (e.g., A=3'b010, B=3'b001) and verify output mapping
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Outputs stable within one delta cycle after inputs settle

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Outputs correctly handle minimum and maximum operand values
Comparator correctly reports transitions at numeric extremes
cases.goal: Check behavior at numeric limits and one‑off boundary conditions
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Maintains value‑oriented coverage without fixed‑latency assumptions

stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['A', 'B']

expected_properties: Outputs track changes without glitches under back-to-back stimulus
cases.goal: Assess output stability when inputs change every simulation delta cycle
notes: No clock is present; stimulus is applied sequentially in time
Helps reveal any unintended latch behavior or race conditions
stimulus_intent: Toggle A and B between contrasting values each delta cycle to expose potential glitches
timing_assumptions: Assume combinational propagation completes within the same simulation timestep; observe outputs after each input change

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: Comparator outputs remain defined (0 or 1) even when inputs contain X or Z bits
cases.goal: Verify robustness against undefined input bits
notes: Although the contract does not specify illegal input handling, exercising X/Z helps uncover unintended X-propagation
